devnotes.summary: Defines core data types and classes for representing state variables, parameters, and external variables in a crop simulation model. Provides a standardized way to handle variables that change over time (states), fixed model parameters, and variables imported from other modules or the environment.
Enumeration type for pointer indirection, important for external state variables and external values, where the name of a state variable can mean either the state itself or its rate of change.

THumeEntity is the base class for all objects representing either numerical values (states, variables, parameters, constants) or Options. It provides basic functionality for variables and external "driving forces". If the destination is a console app it is derived from TObject. For use within the IDE/GUI applications it is derived from TPersistent.
Internal field for the name of the entity.
Internal field for comments/explanation.
Internal field for the name of the Entity.
Internal field for the submodel to which the Entity belongs.
Internal field for the documentation link.
Units of the entity (e.g., 'kg/ha', 'm').
Indicates if the entity is an array.
Lower bound for indexed entities (arrays).
Upper bound for indexed entities (arrays).
Flag to indicate if the value should be initialized from a file.
Flag to control output to a text file.
Flag to control output to an INI file.
Flag for selection for sensitivity analysis output.
Flag indicating if initialization from file was successful.
The name of the object.
The name of the submodule this entity belongs to.
A descriptive comment or explanation for the entity.
A web link to documentation for this entity.
Flag indicating whether to write output to a text file.
Flag indicating selection for sensitivity analysis output.
The physical units associated with the object's value.
Represents a model option with a string value and a list of possible choices.
The default string value for the option.
A list of permissible string values for the option.
The currently selected option string.

THumeNumEntity is an abstract base class for numerical entities in the model, such as variables and external values. It extends THumeEntity with properties for numerical values, units, and flags for plotting and output.
The internal numerical value of the entity.
The default value, often used for initialization.
Flag to control graphical output (plotting).
Flag for selection to a global output file.
Flag for extra output of final values in a file.
Flag to indicate if measured data is available for this entity.
Writes the declaration code for this entity to a source file.
Writes the property declaration code for this entity to a source file.
Writes the creation/instantiation code for this entity to a source file.
Property for optional plotting to a graph.
Property for the optional output of final values.
Property for the number of digits used in text output.
Property for the precision (digits after decimal point) of the numerical value.
Property indicating if the entity is written to a global output file.
The main numerical value of the entity.

TVar is a class representing a variable, i.e., a value that can be calculated for each time step in the model from other variables, state variables, or parameters. It is the base class for TPar and TState.
String containing source code for rate calculation.
String containing source code for initialization.
Instantiates a TVar (Variable) object.

TPar is a class representing a parameter variable in the model, which can be selected for optimization or sensitivity analysis. It extends TVar and adds properties for min/max values, uncertainty, and selection flags.
Flag to indicate if the parameter is selected for optimization.
Flag to indicate if the parameter is selected for sensitivity analysis.
An uncertainty or error value associated with the parameter.
Instantiates a TPar (Parameter) object.
Property for selecting the parameter for sensitivity analysis.
The maximum expected value, e.g., for optimization routines.
The minimum expected value, e.g., for optimization routines.
TState is a class representing a state variable in the model, which has an initial value and a rate of change. It is integrated over time.
The initial value of the state variable.
The current rate of change of the state variable.
Instantiates a TState (State Variable) object.
TExternV represents an external variable in the model. Values of model elements outside a submodel are "external" and made accessible by linking to the source variable, often via a pointer.
If true, the variable is found by searching the model by name.
Specifies whether to link to the state or its rate of change.
A conversion factor to apply to the external value.
The source of the external variable (e.g., a file or submodel name).
Delivers the external "value" result.
devnotes.returns: The value pointed to, adjusted by the conversion factor.

devnotes.summary: A direct pointer to the external data.
Instantiates a TExternV (External Variable) object.
devnotes.param: Type of external value (state or rate).

devnotes.summary: Sets the internal pointer to the external "value" variable.
devnotes.param: The new pointer to the external data.

devnotes.summary: Specifies whether to link to the state or rate field.
If true, the variable is located by searching the model by its name.
A conversion factor applied to the value when read.
A string identifying the source of the variable.
